name: Classic Puerto Rican Tacos (Ground Beef)
description: Ground beef seasoned with Puerto Rican-inspired flavors and served taco-style in tortillas with fresh toppings.
ingredients: 1 small bell pepper, finely chopped (green or red)
Sliced olives, capers, or raisins for added Puerto Rican flair (optional)
Grated cheese (queso fresco or cheddar work well)
steps: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add onion and bell pepper; sauté 2-3 minutes until softened. Add garlic and sauté 30 seconds until fragrant.
Place ground beef in skillet. Break apart with spatula and cook until browned and cooked through.
Stir in tomato paste, cumin, oregano, paprika, salt, and pepper. Add olives, capers, or raisins if desired.
Pour in water and stir well. Reduce heat to low and simmer 5-7 minutes, allowing flavors to meld. Adjust seasonings as needed.
Warm tortillas in a dry skillet or microwave. Assemble tacos by placing beef filling in center of each tortilla. Top with lettuce, tomatoes, avocado, sour cream, and cheese.
